steps.title: Map the research methods
steps.content: Ferraro uses centuries-old cooking ingredient lists to identify historical fruit varieties. The next sentence adds another source, Renaissance paintings, that serves the same research goal.

steps.title: Reject non-additive transitions
steps.content:
• Choice A: In sum would signal a summary, not another method.
  • Choice B: Instead would falsely replace the ingredient lists with paintings.
  • Choice C: Thus would make the paintings a result rather than an added source.

steps.title: Select the additive signal
steps.content: The transition must show that analyzing paintings is one more way Ferraro researches past fruit varieties in Umbria. The answer is D.
